title: LIDAR Time-of-Flight — Point Cloud Distance Measurement
content: LIDAR (Light Detection And Ranging) measures distance by time-of-flight: d = c·Δt/2, where c=3×10⁸ m/s and Δt is round-trip light travel time. Rotating LIDAR scans 360° building a 2D/3D point cloud.
extended_content: Types: 1) Single-point TOF (TFmini, SICK): 1D range 0.3–12 m, 100 Hz. 2) Rotating 2D (RPLidar A1): 360°, 8000 samples/s, 0.15° resolution, 6 m range. 3) Solid-state (Intel RealSense L515): MEMS mirror, no rotating parts. Point cloud density: N_points/revolution = scan_freq / motor_RPM × 60. Proximity sensing: obstacle detected when point cloud point distance < threshold (e.g. 0.3 m). Drone altitude hold: downward LIDAR measures height, feeds PID altitude controller. SLAM mapping: iterative closest point (ICP) algorithm aligns successive scans. Beam divergence: 0.5–2°; minimum detectable object = beam_width at range.
units: d: m, Δt: s, c: 3×10⁸ m/s
source: Velodyne HDL-64E Technical Manual; Hecht, Optics, 5th ed.; Thrun et al., Probabilistic Robotics, MIT 2005
